Director of National Intelligence Chief Gabbard Is Stepping Down
items.summary: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard says she is resigning to help her husband fight a rare form of bone cancer. Her last day is expected to be on June 30. She's been increasingly out of step with the White House because of her anti-war views. Bloomberg's Jeff Mason reports.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title:
I thank the heads of the communities in the Rivne, Volyn, and Zhytomyr regions for taking such good care of your people and our state. The main focus of our meeting was security in the northern direction. We must remain strong, so reinforcing our defense, building fortifications, and ensuring our troops are ready for any scenario is a priority.

We also discussed support for education and children. There is a clear task for government officials — to ensure free hot meals for all schoolchildren starting from September 1. The issue of building new schools and completing those started before the full-scale invasion will be worked through, especially in communities with high birth rates, along with providing educational institutions with school buses. Road reconstruction remains an important topic. The Government has been instructed to speed up this work.

I am grateful to every community for your resilience and for finding solutions to develop and protect your people even in such difficult conditions.
items.summary:
I thank the heads of the communities in the Rivne, Volyn, and Zhytomyr regions for taking such good care of your people and our state. We must remain strong, so reinforcing our defense, building fortifications, and ensuring our troops are ready for any scenario is a priority.

We also discussed support for education and children

Global Long Bond Yields Hit Highest in Almost Two Decades
items.summary: Subadra Rajappa, head of US rates strategy at Société Générale, joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Real Yield." The selloff in longer-maturity government bonds has pushed up yields to levels last seen during the global financial crisis, and market participants are warning the move has room to run. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Warsh Most Hawkish Fed Nominee in 20 Years: Desai
items.summary: Sonal Desai, CIO of Franklin Templeton Fixed Income, joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Real Yield." Kevin Warsh, who has promised the biggest shakeup in decades at the US central bank, was sworn into office Friday in a White House ceremony as the 17th chair of the Fed. (Source: Bloomberg)
